Name: 1V
Description: Australian growth-stage VC investing in technology and healthcare companies from Series A through pre-IPO. Writes cheques of $1m–$30m. Backs companies with product-market fit looking to scale into global markets.

Name: 30X30 Scaleup Program
Description: Australia's premier scaleup program on a mission to create 30 tech unicorns by 2030. LaunchVic-backed intensive program for high-growth Victorian startups ready to scale globally.

Name: 77 Partners
Description: Early-stage VC backing frontier tech founders across AI, robotics, energy and quantum. Runs free Y Combinator Insights sessions for Australian founders considering applying.

Name: 808 Ventures
Description: Melbourne-based micro-VC investing up to $250k in technology startups at Seed and Series A. Multi-sector focus within technology. Best suited to Australian tech founders raising initial external capital.

Name: Aboriginal Investment NT
Description: NT-based organisation providing grants up to $100K and business support for Aboriginal entrepreneurs.

Name: ACS (Australian Computer Society)
Description: Australia's largest ICT professional body with 40,000+ members. Innovation hub and accelerator programs for tech startups, mentorship, skills assessment. Owns River City Labs.

Name: ACT Government Grants Portal
Description: Search ACT Government grants and funding opportunities for individuals, groups and organisations that benefit Canberrans.

Name: ACT Innovation Connect (ICON) Grants
Description: Matched-funded grants to kick-start growth of early-stage innovative entrepreneurs and startups in the ACT.

Name: ACT Priority Investment Program
Description: Supporting Canberra innovation precinct development and business growth.

Name: ACTivate Capital
Description: ACT's first ESVCLP. $50M fund backed by ACT Government, UNSW Canberra, Epicorp. Defence, quantum, clean energy, AI, advanced manufacturing focus.

Name: Advance Queensland - Ignite Ideas Fund
Description: Funding for commercialising innovative products and services in Queensland.

Name: AfterWork Ventures
Description: Pre-seed and seed investor for AU/NZ startups. Syndicate of experienced operators.

Name: Agriculture Victoria
Description: Supports innovation in food and fibre industries including agtech startups. Operates research facilities like AgriBio and SmartFarms. Partners on the Hugh Victor McKay Fund and Farmers2Founders.

Name: AgriFutures Funding
Description: Agriculture innovation grants supporting agtech, sustainability, and rural industry startups.

Name: AgriZeroNZ
Description: NZ-based public-private partnership (50% NZ government, 50% private agribusiness) with $191m committed. Exclusively funds tools and technologies that reduce biogenic methane and nitrous oxide emissions from pasture-grazed ruminant animals. Pre-Seed through Series B. Technology must demonstrate significant emission reductions and compatibility with NZ farming systems. Extremely specific mandate — agri-emissions only.

Name: Ai Group (Australian Industry Group)
Description: Leading national industry association representing businesses across manufacturing, engineering, construction, defence, ICT and more. Advocacy, IR advice, training and networking.

Name: AIIA (Australian Information Industry Association)
Description: Australia's peak body for the digital ecosystem since 1978. Hundreds of company members representing 750,000+ workers. Advocacy, networking, education, innovation development.

Name: Airtree Ventures
Description: $1.3B under management. Seed to Series B. Portfolio includes Linktree, Go1, Employment Hero. Offers Open Source VC resources.

Name: Alberts Impact Capital
Description: Albert family impact fund investing in environmental sustainability, mental health, and equality. Early-stage focus on mission-aligned founders.

Name: Alberts Impact Ventures
Description: Australian early-stage VC investing $100k–$1.5m in companies with a clear positive impact across environmental sustainability, mental health and wellbeing, the arts, and equality. Pre-Seed through Series A. Does not invest in companies without an impact thesis aligned to these four focus areas.

Name: ALIAVIA Ventures
Description: California-based VC investing in Pre-Seed and Seed-stage female-founded tech startups in the US and Australia. At least one female founder required. Sectors: HealthTech, FinTech, EdTech, HR tech, and media and entertainment. Fund I closed at AUD $13.5m. Cheque size USD $250k–$750k with follow-on available. B2B and B2C both considered.

Name: Alice Anderson Fund
Description: Co-investment fund for female-founded VIC startups. Investors apply on behalf of startups, not founders directly. 85% equity + 15% grant. Future uncertain due to LaunchVic merger with Breakthrough Victoria.

Name: Allied Legal
Description: Startup-focused law firm specialising in IP, employment, and capital raising for founders and scaleups.

Name: Altered Capital
Description: NZ-based global venture capital and growth equity firm managing NZ$280m+. Backs companies that have already proven product-market fit and are ready to scale across seven themes: Data and Digitisation, AI and Automation, Energy Transition, Alternative Proteins, Circular Economy, Future Health and Wellbeing, and Environment and Sustainability. Series A and beyond — not early-stage.

Name: AMES Australia Settlement Services
Description: Settlement services for migrants and refugees including employment support and business start-up guidance.

Name: ANDHealth ACTIVATE
Description: 6-month intensive accelerator for Victorian digital health and medtech startups ready to scale. 45+ companies supported since 2023, raising $35.7M collectively. Hybrid format with on-site sessions at Melbourne's CHICC.

Name: Antler Australia
Description: Global startup generator with residencies in Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane. 10-week program for pre-idea to early-stage founders. $77M Fund II backing 100+ pre-seed startups.

Name: ANU InnovationACT
Description: 10-week entrepreneurship program for ANU and ACT institution students and staff. Workshops, team formation, and $50K seed grant pool.

Name: Appomate
Description: App development agency specialising in mobile apps, UI/UX design, and MVP builds for startups and enterprises. Offices in Melbourne and Sydney.

Name: ARAIN (National AI Adopt Centre)
Description: Commonwealth-funded National AI Adopt Centre focused on AI readiness for Australian industry. Provides AI maturity assessments, adoption roadmaps, and training for SMEs.

Name: Arbor Capital
Description: Australian patient capital investor providing $500k–$5m to high-growth private technology companies with proven business models. Invests at Series A and growth/pre-IPO stages — not early seed. Focus sectors: SaaS, marketplaces, energy, logistics, and fintech.

Name: Archangel Ventures
Description: Melbourne-based VC investing in pre-seed and seed-stage technology startups across Australia.

Name: ARENA (Australian Renewable Energy Agency)
Description: Federal funding for renewable energy and clean technology innovation, including the $2.5B Future Made in Australia initiative.

Name: Arnotts Technology Lawyers
Description: Specialist IP, cybersecurity, and technology transaction lawyers for tech founders across Australia.

Name: Arowana Partners
Description: Global B Corp-certified investment group with offices in Sydney, Brisbane, London, Dubai, Singapore, and Manila. Invests in and actively operates growth-stage SMEs across education, technology, fintech, electric mobility, sovereign AI infrastructure, and circular economy. Takes a hands-on operational role — not a passive VC. Australian-focused Australasian Growth Enterprise Fund targets expansion-stage businesses.

Name: Artesian
Amount: $50k – $500k seed (VCaaS platform; A$1.4bn AUM across all strategies)
Description: Multi-strategy fund manager with VC, credit, and impact funds. Multiple programs for different stages.

Name: Artesian Capital Management
Description: Specialist impact VC and corporate venture firm managing funds across agrifood technology, clean energy, AI/robotics, and medtech. Invests at Series A through Series C in Australian and global companies. Also manages corporate venture programs for strategic partners. Sector-specific — agrifood, cleantech, and deep tech only.

Name: AS1 Growth Partners
Description: Growth equity investor focused exclusively on late-stage and pre-IPO opportunities (Series D+). Not suitable for early-stage or seed-stage startups. Sector focus and cheque size not publicly disclosed — verify current mandate on their website.

Name: ASCA (Advanced Strategic Capabilities Accelerator)
Description: Federal government initiative accelerating quantum, AI, cyber, and hypersonics capabilities for Defence.

Name: Athletic Ventures
Description: Australian VC fund and co-investment syndicate backed by 500+ elite athletes. Invests at Seed through Series B in technology companies across generalist sectors. Cheque size $1m–$4m. The athletic network provides portfolio companies access to brand ambassadors, distribution, and consumer validation.

Name: Aura Ventures
Description: Sydney-based early-stage VC investing $500k–$2m from Angel through Series A. Sector-agnostic but thesis-driven — each deal must fit a defined investment thesis. Backs ambitious Australian founders early and supports through follow-on rounds.

Name: AusAgritech
Description: National peak body for the Australian agritech sector. Runs the National Agritech Awards Gala, National Agritech Meetup and Agritech Showcase connecting founders, investors and ecosystem players.

Name: AusIndustry
Description: Federal government's innovation programs including the R&D Tax Incentive, offering up to 43.5% refundable tax offset.

Name: Austrade Landing Pads
Description: Austrade's export acceleration program for export-ready Australian tech scaleups. Virtual and in-market bootcamps for key global hubs (US, UK, SE Asia). Free; apply via Austrade.

Name: Australian Business Awards (ABA100)
Description: Categories include Business Innovation, Product Innovation, Start-Up of the Year, AI Innovation, Digital Innovation.

Name: Australian Investment Network
Description: Online platform connecting entrepreneurs with angel investors and private equity. Submit a business plan and get matched with investors.

Name: Australian Network on Disability
Description: Membership organisation connecting employers and businesses to disability inclusion best practices.

Name: Australian Space Agency
Description: Programs and funding supporting Australian space industry growth including Moon to Mars initiative.

Name: Australian Startup VC Directory
Description: Curated database of 150 venture capital firms active in Australia and New Zealand, with details on cheque size, sector focus, investment stage, and how to apply.

Name: Australian Technologies Competition
Description: $100,000 investment package from Scalare Partners. Free entry for tech scale-ups across 12+ sectors. Launch event 10 Mar at Tank Stream Labs Sydney.

Name: Australian Unity Future of Healthcare Fund
Description: Healthcare-only VC investing $250k–$7.5m in Series A through Series C companies targeting healthcare, aged care, and disability services in Australia. Managed by Australian Unity, a mutual company. Strictly sector-specific — companies outside healthcare and social care will not qualify.

Name: AWS Activate
Description: Up to $100K AWS credits for startups backed by accelerators or VCs. Cloud infrastructure, technical support.

Name: Azure Group
Description: Chartered accounting firm specialising in tech startups. Provides accounting, tax, R&D tax incentive and EMDG claims, corporate advisory and capital raising support. Sydney-based with national client base.

Name: Bailador Technology Investments
Description: ASX-listed technology investment fund (ASX:BTI) with a concentrated portfolio approach to B2B, SaaS, marketplace, and tech-enabled businesses. Invests $5m–$20m at Series A through late-stage. Actively involved in operations and board governance. Not a closed-end VC — capital is permanent and publicly listed.

Name: Barayamal
Description: World-leading First Nations entrepreneurship organisation. Events, education, mentoring, and research.

Name: Beachhead Venture Capital
Description: Australian early-stage VC investing $200k–$1m in fintech, SaaS, and blockchain startups at Seed and Series A. Sector-focused — not suitable for founders outside these three verticals. Provides hands-on support through its network of founders and operators.

Name: BHP Xplor
Description: 9-month accelerator for critical minerals exploration. Up to US$500K equity-free funding per company. 21 alumni companies since 2023.

Name: Billfolda Ventures
Description: Australian sector-agnostic VC investing $200k–$2m from Angel through Series A. Backs founders from any industry with scalable technology businesses. Open to first-time founders. Cheque size flexible depending on stage and round structure.

Name: Birchal
Description: Australia's largest equity crowdfunding platform. $218M+ raised, 79K+ investors, 300+ companies funded. Great for consumer brands and community-driven businesses.

Name: Birchal First
Description: Birchal's early-stage program for underrepresented founders including women, Indigenous, regional, and migrant entrepreneurs. Activated $6M+ for early-stage startups.

Name: Biztech Lawyers
Description: Melbourne-based technology law firm serving the startup community with commercial, IP, and corporate advice.

Name: Black Nova VC
Description: Pre-Seed and Seed VC investing an average of $750k in mission-critical, workflow-led B2B software targeting regulated and operations-heavy industries. Not suitable for consumer businesses or sector-agnostic software. Portfolio companies must serve mid-market or enterprise customers solving persistent, unglamorous business problems.

Name: Black Sheep Capital
Description: Australian early-stage VC investing at Seed and Series A in sector-agnostic technology companies. Backs founders with bold ambitions and unconventional approaches. Cheque size not publicly disclosed.

Name: Blackbird Foundry
Description: Free 8-week program by Blackbird Ventures for researchers and academics launching science-led startup ideas. $1K per team plus $5K pitch prize. Must have university or AU/NZ connection.

Name: Blackbird Giants
Timing / Deadline: Sydney: Mar-May, Melbourne: May, Virtual: Aug-Sep
Description: Free 1-on-1 mentoring program by Blackbird Ventures. 4-week intensive with weekly in-person community events. No equity taken. Cohorts in Sydney, Melbourne, and virtual ANZ.

Name: Blackbird Ventures
Description: Australia's largest VC with $10B+ AUM. Pre-seed to growth stage. Portfolio includes Canva, Dovetail, Culture Amp. Hosts Sunrise Festival.

Name: BlueRock
Description: Entrepreneurial professional services firm offering accounting, legal, finance, insurance, digital marketing, and R&D incentives under one roof. Dedicated startup advisory practice.

Name: Boosting Female Founders
Description: DISCONTINUED. Federal Government grants for women-led startups. Program axed in 2024 federal budget with no future rounds planned.

Name: Boson Ventures
Description: Pre-Seed and Seed micro-VC investing $100k–$300k in sector-agnostic technology startups that create real value for society. Invests in Australian and global founders. Does not invest in companies without a clear societal benefit. First-cheque investor.

Name: Brandon Capital Partners
Amount: $2m – $30m+ (life sciences seed to expansion; A$430m Fund 6)
Description: Life sciences-only VC managing the Medical Research Commercialisation Fund (MRCF). Invests from Seed through Series B exclusively in biotechnology, medical devices, and life sciences. Backed by major Australian superannuation funds and medical research institutes. Not suitable for companies outside life sciences and biotech.

Name: Breakthrough Victoria
Description: Victoria's $2B patient capital fund supporting commercialisation of research and deep tech innovation, pre-seed to Series B. Runs the University Innovation Platform ($100M across 6 universities) and Fellowship Program ($7.5M creating 50 startups from researchers, $150K per startup). Focus sectors: advanced manufacturing, agri-food, clean economy, digital technologies, health and life sciences.

Name: Breyer Capital
Description: US-based VC firm founded by Jim Breyer (early Facebook investor). Invests globally including in emerging markets. Sectors: AI, AI Health, Consumer Software, Media and Gaming, FinTech, Enterprise and Data, Security, Defence Tech, and Climate. Early to growth stage. Notable portfolio: Meta, Spotify, Etsy, Grammarly, Epic Games. No confirmed dedicated Australia programme — invests globally on a deal-by-deal basis.

Name: BridgeLane Group
Description: Sydney-based single family office investing across venture capital, agriculture, and real estate. VC activity focuses on technology companies at Seed through Series A, with a sector-agnostic approach across enterprise software, fintech, and consumer tech. Also incubates new businesses. Cheque size not publicly disclosed.

Name: Brisbane Angels
Description: Queensland-based angel network supporting local startups.

Name: Brisbane Lord Mayor's Women in Business Grant
Description: Up to $5,000 for Brisbane women-led businesses for equipment, training, education, or professional services. Includes 12-month development program via Brisbane Business Hub. Over $1M awarded since 2021.

Name: Business Innovation Program NT
Description: NT Government grants plus advisor support for product development and market expansion.

Name: Business Queensland
Description: Queensland's business support portal featuring Advance Queensland initiatives and Mentoring for Growth program.

Name: Business Research and Innovation Initiative (BRII)
Timing / Deadline: No rounds currently open; challenge-based program — check business.gov.au for new rounds
Description: Innovative solutions for government policy and service delivery challenges. Challenge-based funding rounds.

Name: Business SA
Description: South Australia's peak employer body. Runs Business Accelerator Program, SAYES (SA Young Entrepreneurs Scheme) and ENCORE programs connecting founders with mentors.

Name: Business Tasmania
Description: Tasmania's business support portal with Business Growth Loan Scheme and incubator programs.

Name: Business Victoria Grants Finder
Description: Find and apply for Victorian Government grants, funding and financial assistance for businesses, including eligibility criteria and application dates.

Name: Business.gov.au
Description: The Australian Government's central hub for business information, grants finder, registration tools and compliance guides.

Name: Business.gov.au Grants and Programs Finder
Description: Free guided search tool to find grants, funding and support programs from across Australian government. Over 500 grants searchable by industry, location, and business type.

Name: Cardinia Ventures
Description: Sydney-based VC investing in software and hardware companies from Pre-Seed through Series A. Backs ambitious technology founders across enterprise applications, SaaS, edtech, logistics, and AI in Australia, the US, and Southeast Asia. Cheque size not publicly disclosed.

Name: Carthona Capital
Description: VC with ~$360M under management. Pre-seed to Series A in consumer, fintech, AI, and enterprise software. Carbon neutral certified.

Name: Cartier Women's Initiative
Description: International award programme backing women-founded, for-profit businesses with social or environmental impact. Fellows receive USD $30,000 to $100,000 equity-free, plus mentoring, global visibility, and a year-long fellowship. Nine regional awards (including Oceania for Australian founders) plus a Science and Technology Pioneer Award. Requires annual revenue USD $50,000–$5 million, team of 5–250 (including contractors), and no more than USD $2 million dilutive funding raised. Science and Tech track excludes AI applications built on third-party models.

Name: Catalysr
Description: Incubator empowering migrant and refugee entrepreneurs since 2016. 1,045+ migrapreneurs supported.

Name: CBA x15ventures
Description: CommBank's venture builder backing startups for 15M customers. Builds, buys, and invests in digital ventures.

Name: CBR Innovation Network
Description: ACT's innovation hub running GRIFFIN Accelerator, KILN Incubator, and connecting Canberra's startup community.

Name: CERI
Description: WA social enterprise since 2016. 1,150+ graduates, $114M combined valuations. Programs include Entrepreneurial Mindset Bootcamp.

Name: CFO Centre
Description: Part-time and fractional CFO services for growing businesses. Experienced CFOs providing financial strategy, fundraising support, and board-ready reporting.

Name: Chamber of Commerce NT
Description: NT's peak business body established 1957 with 1,200+ member businesses. Hosts 100+ business events annually including networking, training and industry forums.

Name: Cicada Innovations
Description: Australia's leading deep tech incubator with 25+ year track record. 400+ startups, $6.1B+ raised. Custom labs in Eveleigh, Westmead HealthTechHub.

Name: City of Melbourne Business Growth and Impact Grants
Description: Competitive funding for small businesses, startups and social enterprises operating within the City of Melbourne. Four streams: Expansion (new product/market/relocation), Social Impact (B-Corp or social enterprise), Shopfront Occupancy (activate vacant shopfronts for 6+ months), Social Enterprise. Co-contribution option for larger grants. Annual round typically opens October.

Name: CivVic Labs
Description: LaunchVic pre-accelerator connecting startups with Victorian public sector agencies to solve real-world government challenges. Startups build and pilot solutions with government partners.

Name: Clean Energy Finance Corporation (CEFC)
Description: Australian federal government-backed specialist climate investor deploying debt and equity across renewable energy, energy storage, electrification, clean transport, agriculture, and climate technology. Not a traditional VC — co-finances projects and funds at growth and infrastructure scale. Over $24b deployed. A dedicated clean energy innovation mandate covers earlier-stage climate tech.

Name: Climate Tech Partners
Description: Melbourne-based climate-focused VC launched 2023. Targets Series A investments in breakthrough climate technologies: grid tech, low-carbon fuels, and climate adaptation. Backed by Australian Ethical, CEFC, Qantas, and Airbus. $50m debut fund. Climate sector only — founders outside this space will not qualify.

Name: Clinton Capital Partners
Description: Sydney-based capital advisory firm that raises capital for early-stage companies and co-invests its own capital. Covers Seed through IPO across fintech, healthtech, edtech, and software. Access to a global network of approximately 5,000 family offices and institutional investors. Operates on a success-fee model — note this is an advisory firm, not a direct VC fund.

Name: Cohort Innovation Space
Description: Gold Coast Health and Knowledge Precinct launchpad for health, science and tech ventures. Runs LuminaX healthtech accelerator, InvestFest, NextGen pre-accelerator, Founders Club peer learning and monthly Office Hours mentoring.

Name: Common Sense Ventures
Description: Australian family office investing $100k–$1m in early-stage software, media, and SaaS companies. Backs founders at seed stage and earlier, with a preference for being the first outside money in. Actively helps Australian companies expand to the US. Portfolio includes two ASX IPO exits.

Name: Coralus (formerly SheEO)
Description: Radical generosity model offering 0% interest loans to women-led ventures, funded by a community of women Activators.

Name: CORE Innovation Hub
Description: Mining, resources, energy and defence innovation hub with coworking and startup programs. Connects startups with industry partners in the resources sector.

Name: CP Ventures
Description: Sydney-based VC investing globally at Pre-Seed and Seed in highly scalable breakthrough technology companies aligned with Industry 4.0. Looks for founders solving massive global problems with potential for rapid revenue growth. Sectors include enterprise software, SaaS, AI, e-commerce, and marketplaces. Sweet spot approximately $300k; range $100k–$500k.

Name: Creative Destruction Lab Melbourne
Amount: No funding - no fees, no equity
Description: Nonprofit seed-stage program for deep-tech founders hosted by Monash Business School. Part of a global network connecting founders with experienced mentors.

Name: Cremorne Digital Hub
Description: Victorian Government-backed innovation hub connecting startups, scaleups, corporates and investors in Melbourne's Cremorne tech precinct. Coworking, corporate innovation programs, and inbound market support.

Name: Croc Pitch
Description: Northern Australia's largest startup pitch competition, run by the Darwin Innovation Hub. Winner receives up to $1M in Paspalis venture capital. Annual event each September.

Name: CSIRO Kick-Start Program
Description: Matched funding for R&D projects in collaboration with CSIRO. For startups and small businesses with revenue under $10M.

Name: CSIRO ON Prime
Description: Research commercialisation program for deep tech ventures.

Name: CUB (Club of United Business)
Description: Private business club connecting Australia's most innovative entrepreneurs and business leaders. 23,000+ member network across Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane and Parramatta with 450+ annual events and physical clubhouses.

Name: Curtin Accelerate
Description: Curtin University's 8-week startup program with weekly sessions, 2-week mentoring period, and Pitch Night. Separate from the week-long Ignition program.

Name: Curtin Ignition
Description: Curtin University's intensive week-long startup program in Perth. Focus on research commercialisation and early-stage ventures. Scholarships available.

Name: Cut Through Venture
Description: Angel investing platform connecting experienced operator-investors with early-stage Australian startups.

Name: D10x Accelerator
Description: UNSW-led defence tech accelerator providing $350k per startup to develop dual-use technologies.

Name: Darcy Naunton
Description: Individual investor — Partner at Black Nova VC and co-founder of YBF Ventures. Invests personally at Pre-Seed and Seed in B2B SaaS and tech startups. Also Chairman at Tractor Ventures. Not a fund — direct angel/partner-level investments only. CFA-qualified.

Name: Darwin Innovation Hub
Description: Northern Territory's main startup hub running the StartNT accelerator program.

Name: Deakin Renewable Energy Pre-Accelerator
Description: LaunchVic-supported program helping Deakin students and researchers develop business ideas in the renewable energy sector.

Name: Defence Industry Development Grants (DIDG)
Description: Up to $1M matched funding across four streams: Sovereign Priorities, Skilling, Export, and Security for defence startups.

Name: Defence Trailblazer
Description: National defence innovation program funding collaborative R&D between startups, universities, and Defence.

Name: DEI Australia
Description: Diversity, equity, and inclusion charity supporting entrepreneurs and workers with disability.

Name: Devika Ventures
Description: Wollongong, NSW-based early-stage VC investing $100k–$1.5m from Pre-Seed through Series A. Targets AI-first, founder-led startups — particularly non-technical founders — with unique industry insight and early commercial momentum. Active in enterprise, education, government, and entertainment. Makes 3–6 investments per year.

Name: Disability Gateway
Description: Australian Government's central resource hub for people with disability, including business support.

Name: Dragonfly Enviro Capital
Description: Sydney-based impact VC investing $300k–$10m in environmental and climate ventures from Seed through growth. Focus areas: sustainable agriculture, clean energy, circular economy, and nature-based solutions. Companies must demonstrate measurable environmental impact alongside commercial returns.

Name: Earlypay
Description: Invoice financing and equipment finance for SMEs. Turns unpaid invoices into working capital within 24 hours.

Name: Eaton Square Venture Fund
Description: Growth capital investor writing $1.5m–$22m cheques into expansion-stage technology and services companies across Australia and internationally. Also provides M&A and capital advisory services. Suited to later-stage scaleups, not early-stage startups.

Name: Empress Capital
Description: Australian VC investing in AI-native companies at Pre-Seed and Seed. Backs technical founders building foundational AI products. Cheque size not publicly disclosed.

Name: EnergyLab
Timing / Deadline: 2026: applications closed 15 Mar; program 3 Aug – 11 Dec 2026
Description: Australia and New Zealand's largest climate tech startup accelerator and community. Programs for founders from idea to scale-up, plus a dedicated angel network.

Name: Enterprize Incubator
Description: Tasmania's startup incubator with spaces in Hobart and Launceston. Free coworking for early-stage founders, mentoring, and community events.

Name: Entrepreneur's Programme
Description: DISCONTINUED. Former flagship federal business growth program offering advisory and matched grants. Closed to new applications; replaced in part by Industry Growth Program.

Name: Entrepreneurs' Organisation (EO) Australia
Description: Global peer-to-peer network of 18,000+ entrepreneurs with Australian chapters in most capital cities. Monthly forums, learning events, and access to the global EO network. For founders of businesses with US$1M+ revenue.

Name: Equity Mates Media
Description: Financial media company making investing accessible for young Australians. Podcasts include Equity Mates Investing and Get Started Investing.

Name: Euphemia
Description: Family office of Zip co-founder Larry Diamond. Invests in fintech and financial wellness startups, plus climate and female-led ventures.

Name: EverestEngineering
Description: Global software engineering company with Melbourne roots. Partners with startups to build and scale products. Active in the Victorian startup community.

Name: EVP (Equity Venture Partners)
Description: B2B software specialist VC with $500M FUM across 4 funds. Invests $500k-$5M in seed and Series A rounds. Portfolio of 45+ companies including Deputy and Shippit. Offices in Sydney.

Name: Export Market Development Grant (EMDG)
Description: Reimburses up to 50% of eligible export promotion expenses. Tiered grants for new-to-export through to expanding exporters.

Name: Exto Partners
Description: Sydney-based growth investor backing post-revenue technology companies with $2m+ cheques. Invests at Series A and beyond, often leading rounds. Hands-on operational involvement. Sectors include fintech, marketplaces, and enterprise software.

Name: Farmers2Founders
Description: National agrifood innovation programs for producers and regional entrepreneurs. Ideas Program, Hatch and TekFarm streams.

Name: Fearless Innovator Grant
Timing / Deadline: 2026 applications close 14 Aug; recipients announced 6 Nov 2026
Description: SA Government grants for women founders to build and scale businesses. Rebranded from Fearless Innovator Grant to FoundHer Grant Program in 2026. Delivered with Women in Innovation (WINN) and Fearless Females Network.

Name: Federation University Regional Pre-Accelerator
Description: LaunchVic-backed program supporting regional Victorian founders through Federation University campuses in Ballarat, Berwick and Gippsland.

Name: FinTech Awards (Ashurst Startup of the Year)
Description: Australia's premier fintech industry awards including the Ashurst Startup of the Year category recognising emerging fintech companies.

Name: Fishburners
Description: Australia's largest startup community since 2011. Coworking in Sydney CBD, virtual programs, events, and founder support. 4,500+ alumni startups.

Name: Flinders NVI
Description: Flinders University's New Venture Institute. eNVIsion incubator, Venture Dorm pre-accelerator, and student entrepreneurship programs in Adelaide.

Name: Flying Fox Ventures
Description: Early-stage VC investing across Australia and New Zealand. Backed by 250+ active angel investors. Rolling fund structure with quarterly investor intakes.

Name: Folklore Ventures
Description: Seed-to-scale VC partnering with Australian and NZ founders. First cheques from $500K. Known for long-term founder partnerships.

Name: Follow[the]Seed
Description: Global VC fund investing in post-seed startups with proven engagement metrics. Australian arm backs habit-forming consumer and enterprise products.

Name: Foundation for Young Australians
Description: National organisation backing young people (up to age 30) with grants, programs, and advocacy. Young Entrepreneurs program supports youth-led ventures.

Name: Founder Institute
Description: World's largest pre-seed startup accelerator with Sydney and Melbourne chapters. Structured 14-week program for idea-stage founders with global mentor network.

Name: Founders Factory Nature Tech
Description: Global venture studio's nature tech accelerator backed by BHP. Supports startups in mining rehabilitation, biodiversity monitoring, and environmental markets.

Name: Foundr
Description: Melbourne-founded global media and education company for entrepreneurs. Magazine, podcasts, and courses from founders like Richard Branson interviews to startup playbooks.

Name: Full Circle Venture Capital
Description: Sydney-based VC investing in seed-stage software companies across Australia. Focus on B2B SaaS and fintech with hands-on portfolio support.

Name: Fullstack Advisory
Description: Startup-specialist accountants covering R&D tax incentives, ESIC assessments, cap table advice, and CFO services for tech companies.

Name: FundWA
Description: WA-focused angel investment network connecting local startups with WA investors.

Name: Galileo Ventures
Description: First-cheque VC for emerging founders. Invests $200k–$500k at Pre-Seed and Seed in ambitious young and first-time founders across software and technology. Runs a founder community and supports through subsequent raises.

Name: Game Plus
Description: Coworking and community hubs for game developers in Adelaide, Canberra and online. Connects game studios, publishers, and creative tech startups.

Name: Gandel Invest
Description: Melbourne-based family office of the Gandel family investing in venture capital funds and direct startup investments. Backs Australian VC funds and growth companies. Not open to unsolicited pitches.

Name: GBS Venture Partners
Description: Life sciences VC managing $400m+ across multiple funds. Invests $10m–$12m over a company's life in biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, and medical devices. Australia's longest-running dedicated life sciences investor.

Name: Gea Ventures
Description: Sydney-based climate VC backing decarbonisation startups at pre-seed and seed. Focus on energy transition, built environment, and industrial emissions.

Name: Giant Leap
Description: Australia's first 100% impact VC fund. Invests in startups tackling climate change, health, and empowerment challenges. B-Corp certified.

Name: Glitch Capital
Description: Melbourne-based VC investing $1m–$3m at Seed and Series A in B2B software companies across Australia and New Zealand. Operator-led fund with hands-on support for go-to-market and hiring.

Name: Global Investors
Description: Sydney-based investment firm. Unverified entity — limited public information available. Verify current mandate and legitimacy before engaging.

Name: Global Victoria
Description: Victoria's trade facilitation agency helping Victorian businesses export. Trade missions, export advice, and global network of trade offices.
